steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Convert radians to degrees
steps.content:
To convert from radians to degrees, multiply by $\frac{180}{\pi}$.
$$\frac{\pi}{10} \text{ rad} \times \frac{180}{\pi} = \frac{180}{10} = 18^{\circ}$$
So angle G measures 18 degrees.

steps.title: Solve for n
steps.content:
We're told that angle G equals $9n$ degrees. Since we found that angle G = 18 degrees:
$$9n = 18$$
$$n = 2$$
A is correct.
